title: Documentation of DELETE /libpod/play/kube incomplete
body:
### Issue Description

Podman's REST API documentation does not give information about the payload or the options accepted by the [DELETE /play/kube](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html?version=v4.3.1#tag/containers/operation/PlayKubeDownLibpod) endpoint. 
Additionally the description "Remove pods from kube play" of the endpoint is misleading as it deletes all supported K8s resources and not only pods.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

The endpoint itself works as expected, only the documentation is misleading/wrong.

### Describe the results you received

Faulty documentation

### Describe the results you expected

Correct documentation.

### podman info output

```yaml
Not really relevant here, but please:

Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.5.0
API Version:  4.5.0
Go Version:   go1.20.4
Git Commit:   75e3c12579d391b81d871fd1cded6cf0d043550a-dirty
Built:        Sun May 14 22:11:42 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: @krucod3 Interested in opening a PR to fix the documentation?

@rhatdan, sorry, busy with [eclipse-ankaios](https://github.com/eclipse-ankaios/ankaios) at the moment. 🤓 

But just as an idea as sending a body with the DELETE request is kind of awkward. Maybe the POST can return an Id for the created kube manifest and the Id can be used for the DELETE? The Id could be just a hash of the file, although having an internal list of kube manifests could also be beneficial.

It seems that the PR fixed the **misleading** documentation issue (point two), but did not address the **missing** options and payload documentation for the end point which is actually the bigger problem.

Maybe I should have expressed myself clearly:
The [DELETE /play/kube](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html?version=v4.3.1#tag/containers/operation/PlayKubeDownLibpod) documentation does not mention anything about a payload or the accepted options. At least the K8s manifest file used to create the pods and resources needs to be provided with the DELETE call in order to successfully delete the resources. Additionally, the Podman CLI accepts a `--force` option which also deletes volumes. I suppose the `--force` is also available for the REST API, and is just not documented. I don't know what other options are also supported.
